description_long: This object is a robust, octagonal wooden picnic table with integrated bench seating on all sides. The tabletop is made of several wooden planks arranged in a geometric, nearly circular shape, with visible wood grain and some surface wear. Four benches, each also constructed from wooden planks, are attached to the table's frame and positioned at equal intervals, providing ample seating. The supporting structure beneath the table and benches consists of sturdy wooden beams arranged in a cross-braced pattern for stability. The wood appears to be stained a deep brown, with some lighter scuffs and marks indicating outdoor use. There is no visible hole in the center of the tabletop for an umbrella.
description: A sturdy, octagonal wooden picnic table with attached benches and a cross-braced support structure. The table and benches are finished in a dark brown wood with visible grain and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the octagonal tabletop and three visible benches, with the support beams partially visible underneath.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the symmetry of the benches and the tabletop's geometric shape.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the same arrangement of benches and tabletop visible.
description_view_3: Another angled view showing the benches and the cross-braced support structure beneath the table.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by a central wooden post with cross-braced beams extending outward, connecting to the benches and providing stability.
